observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Panlungsod passed a resolution donating the transferred properties to the recipient agencies.
observations.reason:
Based on the schedule of lots, it was verified that the subject lots were already transferred to the recipients and such transfers were reflected in the books.

The City Accountant made an entry in the books to record transfer of lots to the recipients.

observations.managementAction: The City Accountant effected the necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: Some Projects/Programs/Activities as reflected in the Schedule of CIP as of December 31, 2018 were reported in the Report on the Status of  Government Projects/Programs/Activities as completed already.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor through the appropriate mechanism of the City Health Office to optimally utilize the Per family Payment Rate Fund in accordance with the utilization set forth under Philhealth Circular No. 10 series of 2012 to ensure all agency’s constituents a better access to quality health services that are efficiently delivered, equitably distributed, fairly financed and appropriately utilized by an informed and empowered public.
observations.managementAction: Ongoing  implementation of the program
observations.reason: Based on the general ledger of the account, it was verified that the fund was utilized for the year, however, the fund has an unutilized balance of P677,789.48 as of December 31, 2018 since the program is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management record the procurement of supplies and materials to inventory account and the CGSO not to issue directly the entire quantity purchased to the requesting offices.
observations.managementAction:
Supplies and inventories were recorded as inventory upon purchase.

Requisition Issuance Slips were issued upon distribution to requesting offices.
observations.reason: Inventory accounts were maintained in the books in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines of Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I and Section 358 for proper inventory control and monitoring and Section 124 of the NGAS for LGUs by requiring the periodic physical inventory of supplies and submitting the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters respectively.
observations.managementAction: The GSO submitted an inventory report of the office and janitorial supplies.
observations.reason: A physical inventory of supplies was conducted. However, the GSO does not maintain stock cards.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain the required SLC for each item of the inventory and record issuances to end-users upon submission by the GSO of the SSMI duly supported with the approved RIS as basis for adjustment of the inventory to the expense account; and for the CGSO to effect regular reconciliation of the balances of the SC with the SLC of the Accounting Department and to conduct annual physical inventory of Drugs and medicines.

We recommended further, proper coordination of CHO, General Services Office and Accounting Office, for the complete and accurate recording/reporting of issuances of drugs and medicines.
observations.managementAction:
A reconciliation of inventories between the Accounting Office and City Health Unit was made.

The City purchased an inventory system. Phase 2, which will be implemented this 2019,  is expected to synchronize records of different offices.
observations.reason: Physical count was conducted, however, count sheet reflecting result of the inventory count was not provided to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended that the City Health Office:

prepare a report on the DOH issued drugs and medicines based on the Invoice Receipt of Property and reconcile with the records of the DOH;
immediately conduct a physical count on the remaining DOH issued drugs and medicines and report to CAO for recording in the books of accounts; 	and
henceforth, receipts as well as issuances, have to be reported on and submitted to the CAO for complete reporting of inventories pursuant to Section 63 of P.D. 1445 and Section 375 (d) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant recorded the receipt and issuances of these items.
observations.reason:
Based on the journal entry vouchers, it was
verified that some purchases of drugs and medicines, medical supplies and equipment were expensed outright.
A physical count was conducted, however, reconciliation with accounting records has not been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant determine the unexpended/unutilized balance of QRF, DRRM-MOOE, and other funds received by the City intended for supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the 5-year period and transfer the same to the special trust fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the trust fund books before the year-end financial statements are finalized and submitted for audit.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant transferred the unutilized QRF and DRMM-MOOE to the Trust Liability-DRRMF account.
observations.reason: Reviewed the General Ledger and noted that the unexpended QRF and DRRM-MOOE for the year was transferred to the Trust Liability-DRRMF account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer and City Accountant facilitate the conduct of reconciliation of the cashbook and general ledger balances to ensure completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions and the reliability of the total Cash and Cash Equivalent reported. Also, make proper representation with the City’s depository bank for the prompt submission of bank statements which is vital in the preparation of BRS.
observations.managementAction: A reconciliation was made between the Accounting and Treasury Office.
observations.reason: Compared cash book of treasurer with accounting records and noted that all transactions during the year have been reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 which provides for the basic requirements for payment of salaries and wages and to submit the payroll and DTRs as prescribed. Also, we recommend to maintain time and attendance logbook for back-up in case of biometric machine breakdown and as a basis for missing in/out punches in machine-generated DTRs.
observations.managementAction: Updated submission of payrolls and DTRs for 2018.
observations.reason:
Some post-audited payrolls during the year are still not supported with DTRs.

It was noted that the guard maintains a logbook for employees rendering overtime services, but not for daily time-in and time-out of employees during regular workdays, hence, the time-in and out from work as reflected in their DTRs could not be verified whenever the biometric machine breaks down.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of Section 52 of the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act of FY 2017 which provides that deductions from salaries and other benefits accruing to any government employee, chargeable against the appropriation for Personal Services, may be allowed for the payment of an individual employee’s contributions or obligations due and in the order of preference. In no case shall the foregoing deductions reduce the employee’s monthly net take home pay to an amount lower than Three Thousand Five Hundred Pesos (P3,500.00).
observations.managementAction: The Management has not renewed MOA with other lending agencies.
observations.reason: Post-audited payrolls and observed that during the year, many employees still received a net take home pay lower than the threshold provided for under the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act of 2018 amounting to P5,000.00, so the Audit Team is reiterating its recommendation in this regard.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all the concerned offices to submit regularly through the General Services office Monthly Report of Official Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption supported with driver’s trip tickets, Daily Gas Issue record and Fuel Control Card to the Office of the Auditor as required under COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 and the Manual on Audit of Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicle.
observations.managementAction: Travel order & trip tickets are required from drivers.
observations.reason: Post-audited gasoline expenses and verified that claims were supported with travel orders and trip tickets, and that checks issued for payment of gasoline were based on the gasoline station’s monthly billing statements and Monthly Report of Fuel consumption. However, no Monthly Report of Official Travels were attached, hence, there is no basis for the Audit Team to determine if funds allocated for official travels are wisely used or abused.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer update the Certified List of Real Property Taxpayers and coordinate with the City Accountant in order to reflect accurate receivable accounts.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer reconciled balances with the books of the accounting office.
observations.reason: Compared the balance appearing in the financial statements with that of the City Treasurer’s records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management observe strictly the mandates provided under Republic Act No. 9003 which is the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 on the collection, segregation and disposal of wastes.
observations.managementAction: Garbages were strictly not collected if not segregated.
observations.reason: Job orders are still hired to perform the segregation of waste materials and garbage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned City Officials to look into the required funding of the projects prioritized by the CENRO for the procurement of additional recycling and composting facilities.
observations.managementAction: The City was granted with Annex Warehouse by Department of Environment and Natural Resources and composting & shredder equipment by the Department of Agriculture thru the Bureau of Soils and Waste Management.
observations.reason:
Conducted site inspection and verified the existence of the equipment in the landfill.

Products from recycling and composting activities were also present in the site.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the provision of additional garbage trucks preferably new units.
observations.managementAction: The City purchased two units of garbage trucks.
observations.reason: Based on the post-audit of disbursements, it was verified that the City purchased two units of garbage trucks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  instruct the City Treasurer to exert more effort and tap the services of the City Legal Officer in the collection of taxes due and collectible by sending Notice of Delinquency to RPT taxpayers.  Likewise, a committee should be formed to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes. Finally, management should strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code in the collections of delinquent taxes.
observations.managementAction: Issued Notice of Delinquency.
observations.reason:
Verified copies of Notice of Delinquency served to delinquent taxpayers.

Information dissemination regarding payment of real property taxes is part of the City’s
Echoing Government Services Program.

observations.managementAction: PPMP were prepared by the different departments.
observations.reason: The procurement schedule for some projects were not stated in the submitted APP, thus, we recommend that the APP be properly accomplished by inputting all the necessary details.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City officials continue to allocate funds as required by law for the Gender and Development Program to address gender issues and women’s concerns.
observations.managementAction: Continued allocating funds for Gender and Development Programs.
observations.reason: Based on the SAAOB as of December 31, 2018, it was verified that funds were allocated for GAD Programs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a complete physical inventory of all properties of the City, prepare and submit reliable RPCPPE thereon.  Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records and to instruct the General Services Officer to maintain an accurate, complete and updated property records.
We recommended that Management facilitate the titling of all parcels of land owned by the City Government by appropriating funds for the titling of the land.
In addition, the Accounting Office should set up and maintain complete accounting records of real properties and for the General Services Officer to maintain and keep on file complete records of real properties.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive request the Sangguniang Panlungsod to pass a resolution donating the transferred properties to the recipient agencies as basis by the Accounting Office to derecognize the subject transferred properties from the books of accounts of the City. Likewise, it was recommended that the concerned recipient agencies be required to submit to the City Government copies of the duly accomplished Invoice and Receipt of Property and copies of JEVs in taking up the subject properties in their respective books.
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
Effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the proper classification and correct balances of the affected accounts, including the corresponding depreciation and liability accounts;
Regularly furnish copies of accomplishment reports to the City Accounting Office to notify the latter when the projects were completed, as basis in updating the accounting records;
Periodically coordinate and make reconciliation of records to ensure that completed infrastructure projects are timely reclassified into the appropriate asset accounts and computed with the corresponding depreciation and liability accounts.
We recommended that the City General Services Office prioritize the disposal of the unserviceable properties of the City in accordance with existing auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor through the appropriate mechanism of the City Health Office to optimally utilize the Per Family Payment Rate Fund in accordance with the utilization set forth under Philhealth Circular No. 10 series of 2012 to ensure all agency’s constituents a better access to quality health services that are efficiently delivered, equitably distributed, fairly financed and appropriately utilized by an informed and empowered public.

We recommended that Management:
Record the procurement of supplies and materials to inventory account and the CGSO not to issue directly the entire quantity purchased to the requesting offices; and
Strictly observe the guidelines of Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I and Section 358 for proper inventory control and monitoring and Section 124 of the NGAS for LGUs by requiring the periodic physical inventory of supplies and submitting the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of  each year for the first and second semesters respectively.
We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain the required SLC for each item of the inventory and record issuances to end-users upon submission by the GSO of the SSMI duly supported with the approved RIS as basis for adjustment of the inventory to the expense account; and for the CGSO to effect regular reconciliation of the balances of the SC with the SLC of the Accounting Department and to conduct annual physical inventory of Drugs and medicines.
We recommended further, proper coordination of CHO, General Services Office and Accounting Office, for the complete and accurate recording/reporting of issuances of drugs and medicines.
We recommended that the City Health Office:
Prepare a report on the DOH issued drugs and medicines based on the Invoice Receipt of Property and reconcile with the records of the DOH;
Immediately conduct a physical count on the remaining DOH issued drugs and medicines and report to CAO for recording in the books of accounts; 
and
Henceforth, receipts as well as issuances, have to be reported on and submitted to the CAO for complete reporting of inventories pursuant to Section 63 of P.D. 1445 and Section 375 (d) of RA 7160.

We recommended that the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office:
Implement better planning system/scheme to enable proper implementation of the priority projects pertaining to the LDRRMF;
Prepare the prescribed Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 relative to its preparation for efficient implementation and monitoring of the programs, projects and activities funded from the LDRRMF and integration of the same into the Local Development Plan; and
Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior year’s unexpended balance to enable the City to implement projects related to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness within the scheduled timeframe.
We recommended that the City Accountant determine the unexpended/unutilized balance of QRF, DRRM-MOOE, and other funds received by the City intended for supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the 5-year period and transfer the same to the special trust fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the trust fund books before the year-end financial statements are finalized and submitted for audit.

We recommended that the City Treasurer and City Accountant facilitate the conduct of reconciliation of the cashbook and general ledger balances to ensure completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions and the reliability of the total Cash and Cash Equivalent reported. Also, make proper representation with the City’s depository bank for the prompt submission of bank statements which is vital in the preparation of BRS.

We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 which provides for the basic requirements for payment of salaries and wages and to submit the payroll and DTRs as prescribed. Also, we recommended to maintain time and attendance logbook for back-up in case of biometric machine breakdown and as a basis for missing in/out punches in machine-generated DTRs.

We recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of Section 52 of the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act of FY 2017 which provides that deductions from salaries and other benefits accruing to any government employee, chargeable against the appropriation for Personal Services, may be allowed for the payment of an individual employee’s contributions or obligations due  and in the order of preference. In no case shall the foregoing deductions reduce the employee’s monthly net take home pay to an amount lower than Three Thousand Five Hundred Pesos (P3,500.00).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all the concerned offices to submit regularly through the General Services office Monthly Report of Official Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption supported with driver’s trip tickets, Daily Gas Issue record and Fuel Control Card  to the Office of the Auditor as required under COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 and the Manual on Audit of Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicle.

We recommended that Management:
Require the City Treasurer’s Office to submit the Report of Checks issued  and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 43 on the Manual on NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period;
Instruct the City Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the City Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements; and
Require the City Accountant to submit paid vouchers and supporting documents and trial balances within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006.

We recommended that management submit to the Auditor for review and evaluation, copies of perfected contracts and all the supporting documents forming part thereof within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-01. Also, in order that management could be informed promptly of any deficiencies noted so that these could be immediately corrected and avoid delays in post audit.
We recommended that the City Treasurer update the Certified List of Real Property Taxpayers and coordinate with the City Accountant in order to reflect accurate receivable accounts.

We recommended that management  cause the immediate remittance of the unutilized balance of the DAP-DILG Fund to the Bureau of the Treasury in compliance to COA Memorandum No. 2015-005 dated March 30, 2015 and the Supreme Court Decision on DAP.
Also, we recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the utilization and liquidation of the fund and proper recording of the transferred funds.  Coordinate with the source agencies for the transfer of the unexpended balance to the General Fund of the City,  if the disposition thereof is not stipulated in the  MOA.

We recommended that Management:
Observe strictly the mandates provided under Republic Act No. 9003 which is the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 on the collection, segregation and disposal of wastes.
Require the concerned City Officials to look into the required funding of the projects prioritized by the CENRO for the procurement of additional recycling and composting facilities.
For the provision of additional garbage trucks preferably new units.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the 20% CDF by implementing programmed projects that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development of the City and its people. Also, strict adherence with the provisions of the Joint Circular pertaining to charges from the CDF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Treasurer to exert more effort and tap the services of the City Legal Officer in the collection of taxes due and collectible by sending Notice of Delinquency to RPT taxpayers.  Likewise, a committee should be formed to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes. Finally, management should strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code in the collections of delinquent taxes.
We recommended that the City officials continue to allocate funds as required by law for the Gender and Development Program to address gender issues and women’s concerns.

We have recommended to Management the following:

a.Conduct the required complete annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end.  This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed assets and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments; and to pin point responsibility/ accountability/ liability for missing PPEs.

Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, AREs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.
